name: California Check Cashing Stores
description_short: California Check Cashing Stores provides check cashing, payday loans up to $250, Western Union transfers, bill pay, and tax prep across multiple California locations.
description_long:
California Check Cashing Stores is a multi-location financial services chain operating across California, incorporated as California Check Cashing, LLC. With at least three San Francisco locations (Mission Street, Market Street, and a second Mission Street branch), the company targets underbanked and unbanked consumers who need accessible, in-person financial services. The company has established a regional presence as a storefront alternative to traditional banking.

The company offers a range of money services under one roof. Core offerings include check cashing, payday loans (marketed as payroll advances) up to $250, Western Union money transfers and money orders, bill payment, Green Dot Visa® prepaid debit cards, reloadable debit cards, and in-store tax preparation. Payday loan applicants need a government-issued ID, Social Security number, checking account details, proof of income, and must be at least 18 years old. Loan applications can be started online.

California Check Cashing Stores distinguishes itself through its multi-service convenience model — customers can cash a check, pay bills, send money internationally via Western Union, pick up a prepaid card, and file taxes all in a single visit. Weekday hours run 9 AM to 7 PM Monday through Friday, which is longer than many competing storefront services. The company operates a customer email contact form and advertises response within 24 hours.

Honestly, this is a high-cost financial services provider by design. The company's own consumer notice states that small-dollar loans used over a long period of time can be expensive. Rates and fees are not disclosed on the website — the site directs consumers to 'see store for details.' Payday advances are capped at $250, making this unsuitable for anyone needing meaningful emergency funds. Sunday hours are unavailable, and Saturday closes at 4 PM. This is a last-resort or convenience option for people without bank access, not a cost-effective choice for those with banking alternatives.
address: 384 Placerville Dr ste e, Placerville, CA 95667

company_info.headquarters: 384 Placerville Dr ste e, Placerville, CA 95667

pros: Multiple San Francisco locations within close proximity for accessibility
Extended weekday hours (9 AM–7 PM Monday–Friday)
Western Union money transfers and money orders available in-store
Online loan application option to start the process before visiting
Tax preparation services available alongside financial services
Green Dot Visa® Debit Cards and reloadable debit cards for unbanked consumers
Bill pay services bundled with check cashing in one stop
cons: Payday loans capped at $250 — too small for meaningful emergency expenses
Rates and fees are not disclosed online; consumers must visit a store for details
Closed Sundays; Saturday hours limited to 10 AM–4 PM
Company's own notice warns that small-dollar loans used long-term can be expensive
Services limited to California locations only
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers needing immediate check cashing without a bank account
People who need to send money internationally via Western Union
Consumers needing a small cash advance of up to $250 before their next paycheck
Low-income filers seeking in-person tax preparation assistance
diagnosis: California Check Cashing Stores is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers in California who need immediate check cashing, small cash advances up to $250, or bundled in-person financial services like bill pay and Western Union transfers. The main caveat is that fees are not disclosed online and payday loans are capped at $250 — consumers with bank access will find cheaper alternatives elsewhere.
services: In-store customer service and loan processing
